explanation:
**Kitchen temperatures** follow international standards, combining the metric system with regional culinary traditions. Recipes from different countries use different temperature scales—Celsius, Fahrenheit, or British gas marks. Understanding these conversions is essential for cooking success.

## How to Convert Oven Temperatures

180°C = gas mark 4 = 350°F. These conversions follow widely accepted culinary standards used by professional chefs and home cooks worldwide.

This calculator applies these standard conversions with the values you enter, verifying typical ranges and alerting you to unusual values.

## Final Notes

This calculation is a reference guide. For critical decisions (food safety, professional cooking), consult with a chef or culinary expert. All values are current as of 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.q: What is 180°C in gas mark and Fahrenheit?
faq.a: 180°C equals gas mark 4 and 350°F. This is a standard baking temperature used worldwide.

faq.q: How do I convert Celsius to Fahrenheit for ovens?
faq.a: Use the formula: °F = (°C × 9/5) + 32. For example, 200°C = 392°F. Our calculator does this instantly.

faq.q: What's the difference between gas mark and electric oven temperatures?
faq.a: Gas marks (1-9) and electric ovens (in Celsius/Fahrenheit) represent the same heat levels. A gas mark 4 oven equals roughly 180°C or 350°F, whether you use gas or electric.

faq.q: How accurate are these oven temperature conversions?
faq.a: These conversions are based on standard culinary formulas and are accurate to within 5-10°F (3-5°C) depending on your oven's calibration.

faq.q: Can I use this converter for all types of ovens?
faq.a: Yes, these conversions apply to standard gas ovens, electric ovens, convection ovens, and most modern kitchen appliances.

faq.q: Why do different countries use different oven temperature scales?
faq.a: Historical and regional traditions. The UK uses gas marks, the US typically uses Fahrenheit, and most of Europe uses Celsius. All represent the same heat levels.

faq.q: Is this calculator free to use?
faq.a: Yes, all our calculators are completely free with no registration required.
No. All calculations happen in your browser. We never send your data to any server.
